blockingIssues.description: resolve_crypto_context only reads from the manual resolver registry and returns CryptoError::ResolverNotFound when no mapping was pre-registered. There is no lazy default-provider initialization, no OnceLock-backed per-model reuse, and no code here that consumes SensitiveFieldMetadata plus global defaults to auto-create a CryptoContext.

The generated encrypt_with_runtime_resolver/decrypt_with_runtime_resolver implementations directly call the runtime resolver for each field but do not auto-ensure readiness first. Because no generated OnceLock/ensure_crypto_ready() path exists, direct runtime-resolver operations still fail unless the caller has already registered contexts manually.

The runtime test suite still asserts that calling encrypt_with_runtime_resolver without prior registration returns CryptoError::ResolverNotFound. This contradicts the feature contract that direct runtime-resolver paths should auto-ensure readiness using defaults/overrides.

Integration coverage still depends on install_sensitive_test_contexts() to pre-register crypto contexts before Store operations. The reviewed tests verify manual setup works; they do not verify the promised behavior that save/get/list/save_many succeed without business-layer registration.

The new process-wide default crypto configuration APIs (default_crypto_config, set_default_crypto_config, etc.) are not consumed anywhere in production code. Outside their own test, there are no call sites in core/src or macros/src, so changing these defaults has no effect on encryption behavior. That falls short of the feature description's required default provider strategy surface for later runtime consumption.

Generated SensitiveFieldMetadata stores only per-field overrides and leaves fields without explicit attributes as service: None and account: None. Because the generated metadata never incorporates the process-wide defaults, later runtime auto-registration cannot discover an effective service/account pair from this hook surface alone, contradicting the stated goal that the generated metadata/hooks be what later runtime auto-registration consumes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify scrutiny/rust worker procedures for verification-only or inherited-code sessions so workers must verify that claimed commits actually implement the promised behavior, and distinguish fail-then-fix expectations from pure verification passes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both reviews flagged mismatch between worker procedure and inherited-code validation; one worker reported success for runtime auto-registration even though current code and tests still require manual registration.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a library note claiming SECURE_FIELDS contains fully resolved default service/account values.
rejectedObservations.reason: wrong: reviewer evidence shows current metadata only preserves explicit overrides, so documenting it as resolved defaults would be inaccurate.
